steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize Sirius A's mass. The correct sentence must name Sirius A and make its mass the focal point.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The mass of stars, like Proxima Centauri, can be described in units called solar masses. This choice discusses solar masses in general terms without naming Sirius A as the subject. ✗
  • Choice B — In astronomy, the mass of stars can be described in units called solar masses, and one solar mass is roughly equal to the mass of the Sun. gives general context about solar masses without naming Sirius A, so it does not emphasize Sirius A's mass. ✗
  • Choice C — The Sun is more massive than Proxima Centauri, which has a mass of 0.122 solar masses. omits Sirius A entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — With a mass of 2.063 solar masses, Sirius A is more massive than the Sun. This choice names Sirius A and foregrounds its mass of 2.063 solar masses, emphasizing that it is more massive than the Sun. ✓
That matches Choice D — With a mass of 2.063 solar masses, Sirius A is more massive than the Sun.
